questions.question: State and prove Theorem 10.1.
questions.answer: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact.

questions.question: State and prove Theorem 10.2.
questions.answer: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

questions.question: Prove that tangents drawn at the ends of a diameter are parallel.
questions.answer: Both tangents are perpendicular to the same diameter, hence they are parallel.

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular at the point of contact passes through the centre.
questions.answer: The perpendicular to the tangent at the point of contact is the radius, which passes through the centre.

questions.question: Prove that the lengths of tangents from an external point are equal using Pythagoras theorem.
questions.answer: Using right triangles formed by joining the centre, the lengths of tangents are shown equal.

questions.question: Explain the angle between two tangents drawn from an external point.
questions.answer: The angle between the two tangents is supplementary to the angle subtended at the centre.

questions.question: Prove that a chord touching the inner circle is bisected at the point of contact.
questions.answer: The radius drawn to the point of contact is perpendicular to the chord, hence it bisects the chord.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral circumscribing a circle satisfies AB + CD = AD + BC.
questions.answer: Using properties of equal tangents from external points, the result is proved.

questions.question: Prove that opposite sides of a circumscribed quadrilateral subtend supplementary angles at the centre.
questions.answer: Using tangent properties and angle relations, supplementary angles are obtained.

questions.question: Explain the geometric rigidity of tangents.
questions.answer: Tangents follow fixed perpendicular and congruence relationships, making them rigid.

questions.question: Prove that the centre lies on the angle bisector of two tangents.
questions.answer: Because the two tangents drawn from an external point are equal.

questions.question: Explain why tangent problems always involve right triangles.
questions.answer: The radius and tangent always form a right angle.

questions.question: Explain why tangent cannot pass through the centre of a circle.
questions.answer: The centre lies inside the circle, whereas the tangent touches only the boundary.

questions.question: Explain tangent properties using the wheel and ground example.
questions.answer: The wheel touches the ground at exactly one point, forming a tangent.

questions.question: Explain why perpendicularity is fundamental in tangents.
questions.answer: Perpendicularity defines the tangent–radius relationship.

questions.question: Explain why tangent geometry is solvable using congruence.
questions.answer: Congruence ensures equality of tangent lengths and angles.

questions.question: Explain why tangent contact is always unique.
questions.answer: Only one perpendicular can be drawn at a point.

questions.question: Explain the role of the centre in tangent constructions.
questions.answer: The centre provides equal radii and right angles.

questions.question: Explain why tangent never intersects the interior of the circle.
questions.answer: A tangent only touches the boundary at one point.

questions.question: Explain why tangent theory is fundamental in circle geometry.
questions.answer: It explains contact, boundary and construction behavior.

questions.question: Explain the application of tangent properties in constructions.
questions.answer: Tangents are used in drawing accurate geometric figures.

questions.question: Explain why equal tangents imply symmetry.
questions.answer: Equal lengths from an external point create symmetrical figures.

questions.question: Explain why tangent proofs rely on right angles.
questions.answer: Right angles simplify geometric relationships.

questions.question: Explain the importance of tangents in real-life applications.
questions.answer: Tangents explain motion, contact and design in engineering.

questions.question: Explain the overall importance of studying tangents to a circle.
questions.answer: Tangents form the foundation for advanced circle geometry.
